entries.description: Confirmed prerequisites at session start: Set 047 closed 2026-05-26 and Set 048 closed 2026-05-27 (both spec-required predecessors). Operator answers via AskUserQuestion: (1) NTE budget $10 for Set 049 (matches Set 048 pattern), (2) Audit-S1 feature roll-call surfaced inline rather than batched at end (operator-accountability mechanism added 2026-05-27 to the Set 049 stub memory).

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped from not-started to in-progress; orchestrator block populated. Note: a Codex hook fired during the session and rewrote the orchestrator block to engine=codex/model=gpt-5.4/effort=medium mid-work — exactly the unreliable-data pattern that triggered Set 049 to begin with. Left as-is per operator-locked premise P3 (Set 049 rip-out will fix this).

Pre-audit survey grounded the audit in 4 areas: (1) read-sites for the 3 dropped fields — 18 Python files + ~20 TS files reference chatSessionId/checkedOutAt/lastActivityAt; all readers outside the coordination path are themselves retiring. (2) joiner conflict-kinds — surveyed conflicts.py to surface the discovered collisions D1/D2/D3. (3) hook + CLI invocation path — claude-session-start-invoker.js currently passes --chat-session-id from SessionStart payload + hard-codes engine/provider + recovers model/effort from prior block. (4) coordination enforcement gate — DABBLER_ENFORCE_CHECKOUT_COORDINATION env var disables H3 refusal by default since Set 046.

Pass A authored proposal.md at docs/proposals/2026-05-27-set-049-orchestrator-coordination-removal/ covering operator-locked premises P1-P5, open topics T1-T7, discovered collisions D1-D3, read-site survey, and 5 feature roll-call items. Pass A routed to gemini-pro (primary author framing: agreeing with the operator that rip-out is right, focused on disposing topics). Cost $0.0175. Result at pass-a.md: v4-compatible (T1), accept-and-ignore (T2), retire writer-log (T5), retire emit (T6), retire all 3 conflict detectors with writer-bypass decoupled+kept (D3), all 5 FRs Keep (1 Defer for FR2).

Pass B (devil's advocate) initially routed to gpt-5-4 which timed out (read operation timeout after 3 attempts) — matching gpt-5-4-pivot-to-gemini memory pattern. Fallback chain ran gpt-5-4-mini first, which succeeded. Cost $0.0300. Result at pass-b.md: counter-positions on T1 (prefer v5 bump), T2 (accept-with-warning), T4 (don't rely on readers-ignore-extras), T5 (keep writer-log), T6 (compat window), session arc estimate 5-7 not 3-5. Identified survey gap (non-runtime consumers: types files, JSON schemas, consumer-repo hooks).

Authored verdict.md synthesizing Pass A + Pass B. Pass A won T1/T6, Pass B won T2/T4/T5, both agreed on T3/T7/D1/D2/D3, session arc synthesis at 5 (Pass B additions folded into S3 sweep+normalize rather than a 6th session). Cumulative routed spend $0.0475 / $10 NTE (0.48%).

Operator inline decisions via 2 AskUserQuestion batches (4 + 4 max-per-call): all 5 FRs Keep (FR1/FR2/FR3/FR4/FR5), T1 v4-compatible (confirmed), T2 accept-with-warning (Pass B won), T5 keep provisionally (Pass B won). 4 of 5 FR answers followed the Recommended path (Pass A position). The accountability mechanism added scope-discipline without forcing material spec changes.

Authored docs/session-sets/049-orchestrator-coordination-removal/spec.md replacing the STUB. Sections: operator-locked premises (P1-P5), audit-locked dispositions (T1-T7 + D1-D3), feature roll-call (5 Keep), §4 survey gap to close before S2 code-deletion, §5 session arc (5 sessions with per-session theme breakdown), §6 non-goals, §7 cross-references. Added Session Set Configuration YAML block (totalSessions: 5, prerequisites, requiresUAT: true, tier: full). Pre-populated session-state.json sessions[] with 5 entries (S1 in-progress + S2-S5 not-started placeholders matching the locked arc).

Operator surfaced two Set-048-era context-menu defects during S1 close-out review: (1) 'Copy Eval ▸' submenu label inaccurate because it contains non-evaluation entries like 'Start Next Session' — renamed user-visible to 'Copy Prompt ▸' (internal copyEval identifier preserved). (2) dabblerSessionSets.copyStartCommand.parallel command existed in copyCommand.ts but had no ActionRegistry entry — added 'Start New Parallel Session' submenu entry with new buildStartNextParallelSessionPrompt helper + dabbler.copyStartNextParallelSessionPrompt registration. Test coverage: ROW_ACTIONS membership bumped 14→15, new gating tests, copyPromptCommands tests for parallel-variant. 668 TS pass + 2 pre-existing failures unrelated.

Two commits: a520b97 (audit S1 artifacts + locked spec + state file) and e32dd85 (context-menu hygiene patch). Pushed to origin/master (5024677..e32dd85). Memory entry written at project_set_049_s1_audit_locked.md; predecessor project_set_049_stubbed.md marked SUPERSEDED in MEMORY.md index. close_session ran clean.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped session 2 to in-progress; orchestrator block populated (still in pre-rip 7-field shape via the legacy writer at this point — rewritten to 4-field omit-null mid-session once the writer reshape landed).

§4 pre-flight survey closed the Pass B gap across 4 non-runtime areas: (1) sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts — ConflictKind Literal + RowPayload.accordionUpdatedAt vestigialities defer to S4 (Extension TS cleanup), not blockers. (2) HarvestService.ts — parseConflicts silently filters unknown kinds, graceful degradation after S2 narrows the joiner CLI output. NOT BLOCKING. (3) Consumer-repo hooks (platform / harvester / homehealthcare-accessdb) — grep for chat-session-id and chatSessionId returns zero matches in all three; T2 accept-with-warning ships as safety blanket. (4) dump_session_state_schema.py — confirmed hit (build_example_state emits the 3 retiring fields; committed reference would drift). Added inline to S2 scope as a trivial addition. Findings written to docs/session-sets/049-orchestrator-coordination-removal/s2-survey-findings.md.

register_session_start signature changes: orchestrator_chat_session_id parameter dropped; orchestrator_model and orchestrator_effort made Optional[str] = None (no 'unknown' default). Orchestrator-block construction rewritten as 4-field omit-null dict per P2: {engine} plus conditional provider/model/effort keys when caller supplied a value. The prior_orch_for_holder_check derivation, v3+v4 same-holder re-attach branches, chat_session_id_matches predicate, and checked_out_at/lastActivityAt=now emission lines all dropped. Comment block citing Set 033 H3 + Set 036 Q5 replaced with Set 049 P1-P3 spec citation. Live writer smoke test confirms the 4-field shape (subset call with engine only renders {engine: 'claude'}).

ai_router/start_session.py rewritten end-to-end. Removed: constants EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT/EXIT_READ_ONLY/CHAT_SESSION_ID_ENV_VAR/ENFORCE_COORDINATION_ENV_VAR; helpers _coordination_enforced/_identity_label/_prompt_takeover_choice/_is_interactive_tty/_log_force_override/_resolve_chat_session_id; the ~100-line H3+H4 refusal branch (prior-block check, engine_provider_match+chat_session_id_matches derivation, take-over/read-only TTY prompt, exit paths, force-override audit log on handoff); --force flag (only consumer was H3 force-override). Added: _warn_chat_session_id_ignored (T2 accept-with-warning — stderr deprecation line per supply); _log_session_start (T5 — generic 'start_session ran' record at ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log). Retained: --chat-session-id flag (value ignored by writer); --model and --effort flags as optional with no unknown default (T3).

Deleted ai_router/new_chat_id.py whole-file (no preservation flag per spec §5 S2 directive). Companion ai_router/tests/test_new_chat_id.py also deleted. T2 accept-with-warning at start_session is the sole BC surface; consumer-repo invokers that historically sourced $CHAT_SESSION_ID from new_chat_id --export are not affected because grep confirmed zero consumer-repo callers.

ai_router/close_session.py::_peek_orchestrator_identity drops chatSessionId from its return dict per P3. Docstring + the calling-site comment in _run_close updated: per-session orchestrator block is historical record under v4, not a check-out flag, so the pre-flip read for audit symmetry stays. The closeout_succeeded event payload reduces from {chatSessionId, engine, provider, model} to {engine, provider, model}. Spec §5 S2 'check-in branch removed' was effectively a documentation-only directive — under v4 the writer never nulled the block on close (that was a v3-era behavior), so no code branch needed removal beyond the docstring tightening.

ai_router/joiner/conflicts.py rewritten. Deleted: detect_engine_mismatch (D2), detect_bare_or_stale_touch (D1 bare-touch + D2 stale-checkout-touch), _touches_workspace helper, DEFAULT_ENGINE_MISMATCH_WINDOW + DEFAULT_STALENESS_THRESHOLD constants. ConflictKind Literal narrowed from 4-kind union to Literal['writer-bypass']. Module docstring + detect_writer_bypass docstring reframed as engine-independent writer-discipline check (D3 decoupled from coordination framing per spec disposition). scan_conflicts signature loses claude_root/copilot_root/engine_mismatch_window/staleness_threshold params (joiner CLI was already on the writer-bypass-only subset, no CLI edit needed).

Surveyed for holder_change / checkout_conflict event-type emit sites across the whole repo. The literal event-type strings appear ONLY in test names (about EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT, not the event type), in the audit proposal, and in the spec — they were never emitted by any code path. T6 is therefore documentation-only; no code edits needed. The closeout_succeeded event type continues to be emitted with the engine/provider/model audit payload (chatSessionId dropped at step 6).

ai_router/scripts/dump_session_state_schema.build_example_state() reshaped to emit the 4-field omit-null orchestrator block on the example sessions (chatSessionId/checkedOutAt/lastActivityAt removed). docs/session-state-schema-example.json regenerated via PYTHONPATH=ai_router;ai_router/scripts python invocation matching the conftest shim. Byte-deterministic drift check passes (format_example(state) == reference contents).

Retired (whole-file delete): test_chatsessionid_writer.py, test_checkout_writer.py, test_start_session_takeover_prompt.py, test_new_chat_id.py. Updated: test_joiner_conflicts.py rewritten to cover only writer-bypass + scan_conflicts walks emitting writer-bypass kinds (removed all engine-mismatch / bare-touch / stale-checkout-touch test classes + synthetic_workspace fixture). test_session_state_v4_writers.py::test_in_progress_session_carries_per_session_metadata loses the orchestrator_chat_session_id kwarg + 3-field assertions; added new test_orchestrator_block_applies_omit_null regression test (engine-only subset call → {engine: 'claude'}). test_no_router_backcompat.py: --force-asserting start_session tests replaced with --chat-session-id accept-with-warning tests. Full ai_router test suite: 952 pass + 1 skip + 0 regressions in 224 seconds.

Committed S2 code deliverable (1e5f53c). Net diff: -2916 lines (3266 deletions, 350 insertions) across 14 files. 4 whole-file deletes + 5 modified Python files + 4 test edits + 1 schema example. Survey-findings document (s2-survey-findings.md) committed alongside the close-out artifacts in a separate commit per the S1 pattern.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session for S3 with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped session 3 from not-started to in-progress; orchestrator block populated on the v4 sessions[] entry. The new 4-field omit-null shape (post-S2 writer reshape) is what landed on disk — no chatSessionId / checkedOutAt / lastActivityAt fields written, confirming S2's writer rewrite is operational.

Operator-flagged mid-session: an in-progress chat in dabbler-platform was about to create a new session set following stale state-file-rule guidance (v3-shape completedSessions[] / currentSession references). Surveyed instruction files across 4 repos. Updated state-file rules to v4 sessions[] shape in: this repo's CLAUDE.md § Session state schema; dabbler-platform/CLAUDE.md § State-file rules; dabbler-platform/docs/planning/lessons-learned.md (register_session_start lesson marked RESOLVED with historical context preserved); dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb/CLAUDE.md close-out step. Per operator decision (Canonicalize - recommended option): deleted consumer-repo copies of ai-led-session-workflow.md (812-955 lines behind canonical) and session-set-authoring-guide.md (116-152 lines behind) in dabbler-platform + dabbler-access-harvester; updated 6 consumer instruction files (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / GEMINI.md in both repos) to reference the canonical via GitHub URL matching the established session-state-schema.md pattern. platform-addendum.md kept as legitimate Blazor / MudBlazor / Playwright addendum. Drift detector (extending migrators with known-stale-term warning scan) deferred to a future audit-then-spec follow-on set per operator-locked decision.

Operator-flagged mid-session: dabbler-platform/docs/session-sets/template-scaffolding-followup-fixes/session-state.json showed a 'needs migration' label in the Session Set Explorer. File inspection: it was schemaVersion=2 despite being created today (2026-05-27) by a Codex start_session invocation. Hand-migrated to canonical v4 (3-session ledger with session 1 in-progress under Codex orchestrator). Flagged separately: the v2-emission writer-bug is investigation-worthy because per memory all writers should emit v4 since Set 047 S4 — suggests a stale pip-installed dabbler-ai-router in dabbler-platform's .venv, or a non-router writer code path. Deferred as separate substantive investigation.

tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/scripts/claude-session-start-invoker.js rewritten per spec §5 S3. Removed: extractSessionId + chatSessionId forwarding (H4 composite identity gone); preserveExistingClaude chatSessionId composite logic; EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT constant + handling; CONFLICT_DIR + emitConflictRecord function + its call site; DABBLER_ENFORCE_CHECKOUT_COORDINATION env-var gating; Round-B Low payload-schema-drift stderr signal. Replaced preserveExistingClaude with recoverPriorClaudeModelEffort which returns {model, effort} or {undefined, undefined} based on engine+provider match (no chatSessionId composite). New code respects T3 omit-null contract: when no prior model/effort is recoverable, the args are omitted entirely from the spawn — no 'unknown' fallback. Module exports reduced to {parsePayload, recoverPriorClaudeModelEffort}. Net change: 372 → 226 lines (gross: 314 deletions / 168 insertions).

ai_router/migrate_v3_to_v4.py extended with the T4 sweep+normalize pass per spec §2 T4. New constants: _RETIRED_ORCHESTRATOR_KEYS = (chatSessionId, checkedOutAt, lastActivityAt); SWEEP_BACKUP_FILENAME = session-state.pre-049-sweep.bak.json; ACTION_SWEPT_ORCHESTRATOR = 'swept-orchestrator'. New helpers: _strip_retired_orchestrator_keys (block) returns (new_block, changed) — strips retired keys regardless of value (omit-null contract); _sweep_orchestrator_blocks(state) returns (new_state, changed) — sweeps both top-level legacy orchestrator AND per-session ledger entries. migrate_one_set: v4 fast-path branch enhanced to detect retired keys and either sweep+write (with Set-049-specific backup filename) or skip cleanly; v3→v4 path applies sweep on output. CLI _print_result_line + main summary handle the new action. 15 new sweep tests added to test_migrate_v3_to_v4.py (TestStripRetiredOrchestratorKeys, TestSweepOrchestratorBlocks, TestMigrateOneSetSweep classes). 967 Python tests pass + 1 skip + 0 fail.

tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/migrateSessionStateV4.ts received parity sweep edits per the Python↔TS lockstep contract documented at the module head. Added: SWEEP_BACKUP_FILENAME constant; 'swept-orchestrator' to MigrationActionV4 type; RETIRED_ORCHESTRATOR_KEYS constant; stripRetiredOrchestratorKeys + sweepOrchestratorBlocks helpers mirroring the Python sweep semantics. migrateOneSetV4 v4 fast-path branch + v3→v4 build output both apply the sweep. tsc --noEmit clean. TS tests not added in S3 per spec §5 S4's scope ('drop coordination assertions; keep the invoker shape tests for the simplified path') — S4 will sweep TS test files alongside the TS command retirements.

docs/session-state-schema.md orchestrator-block sections reshaped per spec §5 S3. Per-session orchestrator block table reduced to 4-field omit-null (engine/provider/model/effort) with new 'Omit-null on disk (Set 049 T3)' callout. Check-out / check-in section (~90 lines including H4 holder identity, hard-coordination gate, chatSessionId per-chat sourcing, per-set lifecycle lock, stranded-checkout recovery) replaced with: new § Writer Contract (T3) documenting the per-orchestrator declaration pattern (caller passes what it can declare authoritatively; omit-null on disk; no 'unknown' fallback); § Block-preserved-on-close (carrying forward the v4 historical-attribution contract); § ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log note (T5 keep-provisionally). All JSON examples in the doc stripped of the 3 retired keys (top JSON template, plan-less carve-out, between-sessions worked example, completed worked example). Historical context paragraph 'Historical: chatSessionId / checkedOutAt / lastActivityAt' preserved to document schema-evolution.

Ran migrator dry-run across all 49 historical session sets per spec §5 S3 verification step: 46 v3→v4 migrations (sets 001-046) + 3 v4-sweeps (047/048/049) + 0 would-violate + 0 backup-failed. Verbose dry-run on set 047 confirmed before/after diff: orchestrator blocks went from {engine, provider, model, effort, chatSessionId, checkedOutAt, lastActivityAt} → {engine, provider, model, effort}. Sweep is idempotent: re-running on already-clean state returns SKIPPED_V4. The dabbler-ai-orchestration repo itself has 46 v3 sets still on disk (created pre-Set-047 launch) that the operator can migrate at their discretion via --in-place; not in S3 scope.

Saved project memory entry project_consumer_repo_doc_canonicalization.md capturing the cross-repo state-file-rule corrections + canonicalization decisions (delete consumer copies, GitHub-URL refs) + deferred drift-detector follow-on. MEMORY.md index updated. The memory documents the > **RESOLVED — v4 schema** convention as the resolve-without-deleting pattern for lessons-learned files going forward.

Committed S3 code deliverable (d99bdb7) with 6 files staged: CLAUDE.md, ai_router/migrate_v3_to_v4.py, ai_router/tests/test_migrate_v3_to_v4.py, docs/session-state-schema.md, tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/scripts/claude-session-start-invoker.js, tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/migrateSessionStateV4.ts. Net diff: +862/-393 lines across 6 files. Pushed to origin/master. start_session pre-close writes (session-state.json + session-events.jsonl) intentionally excluded from this commit per the Set 049 S2 pattern — they land in a separate close-out commit alongside activity-log + close-out artifacts.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped session 4 from not-started to in-progress with the post-Set-049 4-field omit-null orchestrator block ({engine, provider, model, effort}); work_started event appended to session-events.jsonl. No --chat-session-id passed (post-S2 the writer ignores it anyway per T2).

Pre-flight survey per spec §4 closed gaps Pass B identified as under-weighted by the original audit. Surveyed sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts (4 retiring types + 2 RowPayload fields confirmed solo-consumed by client.js), HarvestService.ts (sole caller is CustomSessionSetsView.buildRow; load-bearing log-harvest scaffolding lives in Python joiner, not the TS cache wrapper), consumer-repo hooks (none ship custom claude-session-start-invoker), and Gemini/Copilot installer shims (both broken-by-construction post-rip — wrap retired surfaces with no remaining purpose). Two divergences from the spec's literal retire list documented in s4-close-reason.md §Spec divergences: HarvestService.ts deleted (not stubbed) and Gemini+Copilot installer shims deleted (not on retire list but broken post-rip).

Deleted 8 retired source files: src/commands/{checkOutOrchestrator,releaseCheckOut,newChatIdWorkflowToast,installOrchestratorHookGemini,installOrchestratorHookCopilot}.ts + src/providers/{CheckoutPollService,chatSessionMismatchModal,ReadOnlyIntentService}.ts. Plus src/providers/HarvestService.ts (spec divergence — see s4-close-reason.md).

Deleted 13 retired test files: src/test/suite/{checkOutOrchestrator,checkOutOrchestratorChatSessionMismatch,releaseCheckOut,chatSessionMismatchModal,checkoutPollService,readOnlyIntentService,readOnlyIntentTiming}.test.ts + src/test/playwright/{new-chat-id-cli-flow,chatsessionid-takeover,chatsessionid-missing-tolerance,checkout-polling,checkout-conflict,harvest-signals}.spec.ts.

package.json: removed 5 command entries (dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator, dabbler.releaseCheckOut, dabbler.installOrchestratorHook.gemini, dabbler.installOrchestratorHook.copilot) + the dabblerSessionSets.checkoutPollTimeoutMinutes config setting. extension.ts: removed 5 imports + 5 safeRegister calls + the inline CheckoutPollService instantiation block + the ReadOnlyIntentService dispose wiring. Added a new Set 049 comment block documenting the rip and the surviving surfaces.

Set 045 Explorer surface revert per operator-locked P4. CustomSessionSetsView.ts: dropped HarvestService import + instance + cache calls; buildRow no longer attaches harvestSignals/conflicts; dispose/refresh no longer touch the harvest cache. sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts: retired ConflictKind + ConflictSeverity + HarvestSignalsPayload + ConflictPayload types + RowPayload.harvestSignals + RowPayload.conflicts fields. media/session-sets-tree/tree.css: removed .harvest-badges + .harvest-badge* + .conflict-pills + .conflict-pill + .conflict-severity-* rules (~95 lines). media/session-sets-tree/client.js: deleted renderHarvestBadges() + renderConflictPills() functions and their call sites in renderRow() (~50 lines). Row layout reverts to pre-Set-045 shape: name + fraction + description only.

ActionRegistry.ts: removed dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator entry from ROW_ACTIONS (14 entries, was 15); dabbler.openOrchestratorWriterLog retained per T5. actionRegistry.test.ts: updated count assertion 15→14 + expected-id set; replaced 'Set Orchestrator… gated to in-progress' test with 'checkOutOrchestrator fully retired' + new 'openOrchestratorWriterLog stays for all states' test. rowMenuHelpers.test.ts: replaced dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator fixture in the flat-actions test with dabbler.openOrchestratorWriterLog. watcherInventory.test.ts: WATCHER_ALLOWLIST trimmed from 3 entries to 1 (CheckoutPollService's 2 entries removed; extension.ts tree-refresh line number updated from 150 to 141 after the wiring trim); baseline-count assertion 3→1.

claudeSessionStartInvoker.test.ts rewritten to match the post-S3 shim exports. The pre-rip test imported extractSessionId + preserveExistingClaude — both removed from the shim in S3 (the shim now exports parsePayload + recoverPriorClaudeModelEffort only). Without this update the test was broken since S3 (silent failure at suiteSetup import). New suites cover the surviving surface: parsePayload happy/error paths + recoverPriorClaudeModelEffort with the v4 sessions[]-active branch, the fallback-to-last-completed branch, the legacy-top-level-orchestrator branch, and the no-'unknown'-fallback T3 contract.

docs/cross-repo-checkout-notice.md rewritten as the T7 deprecation instruction. The doc now opens with a status banner directing consumer-repo authors to remove the H3 'Orchestrator check-out / check-in' block from their CLAUDE.md (covers both the Set 033 'engine + provider' and Set 036 'engine + provider + chatSessionId' shapes). Body includes step-by-step removal instructions, a 'what survives' list (state-file orchestrator block, --chat-session-id accept-with-warning, writer-log, writer-bypass detector), and a 'what was retired' list (coordination, new_chat_id CLI, checkOutOrchestrator/releaseCheckOut/installer shims, takeover modal + poll service, Set 045 harvest badges + conflict pills).

Ran npm run compile + npm run test:unit + npm run test:playwright. Layer-1: 553 passing, 2 failing (both pre-existing — configEditor-foundation vscode-stub ViewColumn.One gap + notificationsSection s5-test-notification disabled-attribute regex; verified by git-stash comparison against master HEAD before S4 edits). Layer-3: 14 passing, 4 failing (all pre-existing — 3 blocked-by-prereqs.spec.ts FileExistsError races on Windows temp-dir + 1 migration-cta-v4.spec.ts needs-migration badge missing; same stash-comparison evidence). S4 added no regressions; net Layer-1 test-count drop (≈80 tests) matches the test-file deletion footprint.

Operator confirmed S5 version-bump targets via AskUserQuestion: ai_router minor (0.10.0 → 0.11.0), Marketplace minor (0.23.0 → 0.24.0), and YES backfill the ai_router CHANGELOG gap for 0.8.0/0.9.0/0.10.0 entries that were missed during their respective sets. Spec recommendation tracked operator pick on bump magnitude; backfill is a modest scope expansion that restores CHANGELOG integrity before the 049 release.

CLAUDE.md rewrite: 'Hard-coordination enforcement (Sets 033 / 036) is OFF by default' section retired entirely; new 'Orchestrator-block contract (post-Set-049)' section documents the 4-field omit-null shape (engine/provider/model/effort), the historical-record-not-check-out framing, D3 writer-bypass detector survival decoupled from coordination context, P4 Explorer revert, and pointers to docs/session-state-schema.md § Writer Contract + docs/cross-repo-checkout-notice.md. Extension versioning section: v0.24.0 description added (full feature manifest, Set 049 — Orchestrator coordination removal), v0.23.0 demoted to Previous, v0.22.0 to Pre-Previous, v0.21.0 to Pre-Pre-Previous, v0.18.1 to Pre-Pre-Pre-Previous with no content loss in the version walk.

docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md: 'Orchestrator check-out / check-in (Set 033)' section (114 lines) replaced with 'Orchestrator identity and concurrency (post-Set-049)' describing the 4-field record, T3 declaration contract, T2 accept-with-warning behavior with the exact stderr line, ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log retained as generic audit appender, tier symmetry, D3 writer-bypass detector survival, and P4 Explorer revert. Step-8-area cross-reference at ~line 1770 rewritten to point at the new section. Step 6 itself did not cite the coordination layer; spec §5 S5 directive fully satisfied.

PyPI dabbler-ai-router bumped 0.10.0 → 0.11.0 in pyproject.toml. ai_router/__init__.py __version__ bumped 0.9.0 → 0.11.0 (caught a pre-existing skip from Set 048's 0.10.0 release where the module-level constant was never incremented). ai_router/CHANGELOG.md [0.11.0] entry added with full Breaking / Changed / Removed / Kept sections detailing every retired surface and the T2 accept-with-warning compatibility guarantee. Retroactive CHANGELOG backfill: [0.8.0] (Set 045 — log-harvest), [0.9.0] (Set 047 — v4 schema), [0.10.0] (Set 048 — Lightweight parity) entries added from memory + extension CHANGELOG mirrors so the ai_router CHANGELOG matches the live PyPI version history.

Marketplace dabbler-ai-orchestration bumped 0.23.0 → 0.24.0 in tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/package.json. tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/CHANGELOG.md [0.24.0] entry added with the same Breaking / Changed / Removed / Kept structure. .vsix packaged clean at 927.58 KB / 22 files after a .vscodeignore hygiene tweak (added test-results/** + playwright-report/** entries so Playwright run artifacts no longer bloat the published package; ~5 files / 13 KB shaved off the 0.23.0 baseline's 940.42 KB).
